metadata.title: The Use of AI: Hype, Reality, and System Integration
metadata.description: A dialogue graph about the difference between simple AI explanations and the real complexity of system development, architecture, integration, governance, and value creation.

nodes.label: The Use of AI
nodes.info:
## The Use of AI

AI is easy to talk about as a promise, but hard to use well in real systems.

This graph separates the public story of AI from the real work of building, integrating, governing, and maintaining AI inside production systems.

nodes.label: The Hype Layer
nodes.info:
## The Hype Layer

- AI is often presented as if prompts alone are enough.
- Influencers frequently describe outcomes without understanding system design.
- Demos are confused with robust products.
- FOMO creates pressure to adopt AI before the actual use case is clear.
- Simplicity is over-sold because it is easier to market than complexity.

nodes.label: The Simple Story
nodes.info:
## The Simple Story

A simple story about AI can be useful for onboarding:

- ask a model a question
- get an answer
- automate a task
- speed up writing or coding

This is useful, but incomplete.

nodes.label: The Real System Work
nodes.info:
## The Real System Work

Real AI use means building systems that can operate over time.

That includes architecture, reliability, cost control, evaluation, security, governance, user workflow design, fallback behavior, and maintainability.

nodes.label: Architecture and Integration
nodes.info:
## Architecture and Integration

- model selection
- tool orchestration
- API contracts
- data flow
- authentication and authorization
- observability
- versioning
- storage and retrieval
- deployment boundaries
- system resilience

nodes.label: Operational Risk
nodes.info:
## Operational Risk

AI systems can fail in ways that look intelligent.

Key risks include:

- hallucinated outputs
- wrong tool selection
- privacy leaks
- security mistakes
- brittle automation
- unclear responsibility
- hidden cost growth
- false confidence in generated results

nodes.label: Where AI Actually Helps
nodes.info:
## Where AI Actually Helps

AI becomes valuable when it is placed inside a clear system with real constraints.

Examples:

- summarizing complex material
- transforming content between formats
- assisting with first drafts
- supporting search and retrieval
- helping operators move faster inside a governed workflow
- augmenting humans instead of pretending to replace them

nodes.label: Questions for Dialogue
nodes.info:
## Questions for Dialogue

- What part of AI can be explained simply without becoming misleading?
- What should always be described as system work rather than model magic?
- Where does AI help human work, and where does it create new complexity?
- What kind of architecture is required before AI is trustworthy in production?
- How should we explain AI to non-technical people without hiding the real cost of integration?

nodes.label: Visual Direction
nodes.info:
## Visual Direction

A simple visual could split the topic into two clear panels:

### Left side: The easy public story

- prompt in
- answer out
- speed
- automation
- excitement

### Right side: The real production picture

- architecture
- integrations
- governance
- data flow
- security
- evaluation
- maintenance
- cost
- human oversight
